en: And Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two female servants.
zh: 雅各舉目觀看，見以掃帶著四百人來了；他就把孩子分開三隊，交給利亞、拉結和兩個婢女。

en: And he put the servants with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
zh: 他叫兩個婢女和她們的孩子走在前頭，利亞和她的孩子跟著，拉結和約瑟走在最後。

en: He himself went on before them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
zh: 他自己卻走在他們前面，七次俯伏在地，直到走近他哥哥跟前。

en: But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
zh: 以掃跑來迎接他，擁抱他，伏在他頸項上，與他親吻；兩個人都哭了。

en: And when Esau lifted up his eyes and saw the women and children, he said, "Who are these with you?" Jacob said, "The children whom God has graciously given your servant."
zh: 以掃舉目觀看，看見婦人和孩子，就問：“這些與你同來的人是誰？”雅各回答：“是 神賜給你僕人的孩子。”

en: Then the servants drew near, they and their children, and bowed down.
zh: 於是，兩個婢女和她們的孩子上前下拜。

en: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down. And last Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
zh: 利亞和她的孩子也上前下拜，隨後，約瑟和拉結也上前下拜。

en: Esau said, "What do you mean by all this company that I met?" Jacob answered, "To find favor in the sight of my lord."
zh: 以掃又問：“我遇見的這一群牲畜，是甚麼意思呢？”雅各回答：“是要討我主喜悅的。”

en: But Esau said, "I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself."
zh: 以掃說：“弟弟啊，我已經很富有了，你的仍歸你吧。”

en: Jacob said, "No, please, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand. For I have seen your face, which is like seeing the face of God, and you have accepted me.
zh: 雅各說：“請不要這樣，我若得你的喜悅，就求你從我手裡收下這些禮物吧；因為我看見了你的面，就好像見了 神的面；你實在恩待了我。

en: Please accept my blessing that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough." Thus he urged him, and he took it.
zh: 請你收下我帶來給你的禮物，因為 神施恩給我，我甚麼都有了。”雅各再三懇求他，他才收下了。

en: Then Esau said, "Let us journey on our way, and I will go ahead of you."
zh: 以掃說：“我們起程前行吧，我和你一起走。”

en: But Jacob said to him, "My lord knows that the children are frail, and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day, all the flocks will die.
zh: 雅各對他說：“我主知道孩子們還年幼嬌嫩；牛羊也正在乳養的時候，需要我的照顧；如果要催趕牠們走一天的路，恐怕所有牲畜都要死了。

en: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, at the pace of the livestock that are ahead of me and at the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir."
zh: 請我主在僕人前頭先行；我要照著在我面前群畜和孩子們的速度，慢慢前行，直走到西珥我主那裡去。”

en: So Esau said, "Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me." But he said, "What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord."
zh: 以掃說：“讓我把跟隨我的人留幾個在你這裡吧。”雅各說：“為甚麼要這樣呢？只要我得我主的喜悅就是了。”

en: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
zh: 於是，以掃在那一天回西珥去了。

en: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
zh: 雅各卻起程到疏割去，在那裡為自己建造了一座房子，又為牲畜搭了些草棚，因此給那地方起名叫疏割。

en: And Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram, and he camped before the city.
zh: 雅各從巴旦．亞蘭回來的時候，平平安安地到了迦南地的示劍城，在城的對面支搭了帳棚。

en: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, he bought for a hundred pieces of money the piece of land on which he had pitched his tent.
zh: 他支搭帳棚的那塊地，是他用一百塊銀子向示劍的父親哈抹的子孫買來的。

en: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
zh: 在那裡雅各建了一座壇，給它起名叫伊利．伊羅伊．以色列。
